key_points: Official OpenAI announcement: workspace agents powered by Codex run in the cloud for background execution
Teams create shared agents via sidebar: 'Click Agents, describe a workflow, ChatGPT builds it step-by-step'
Agents can write/run code, use connected apps, remember context, and continue across multiple steps
Free research preview until May 6, 2026; credit-based pricing starts then
Available on ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans
Positioned as successor to Custom GPTs with three key pillars: background execution, native org sharing, and real SaaS actions
60+ built-in enterprise connectors (Slack, Google Drive, Salesforce, Notion, Atlassian Rovo, Microsoft apps, SharePoint)
MCP (Model Context Protocol) support for any tools not covered by built-in connectors
Scheduling, admin controls (Compliance API, role-based access, kill switch, prompt injection defense)
ChatGPT Business at $20/user/month; variably priced Enterprise/Edu/Teachers
Four essentials: background autonomy, team sharing, enterprise integrations, and admin governance
Unlike Custom GPTs (scoped prompts), workspace agents are autonomous, schedulable, and 24/7
Skills system: reusable packages of instructions, resources, and scripts for reliable agent behavior
Off by default for Enterprise at launch; requires admin activation
Not available for Enterprise customers using Enterprise Key Management
Step-by-step creation: describe workflow or upload file, ChatGPT defines steps and connects tools
Design framework: clarify responsibilities, triggers, stop conditions, tools, process, and rules
Skills can be uploaded or built in-place via the built-in skill builder
Agents interact via ChatGPT and Slack; more surfaces coming soon
Slack integration lets agents live inside channels, respond to @mentions, own tickets
Developer cookbook: building workspace agents for repeatable, end-to-end workflows
Example: sales meeting prep agent queries CRM, drafts briefing, sends to Slack before each meeting
Other cited examples: triaging internal software requests, routing product feedback, qualifying sales leads, booking reconciliation
Finance use case: weekly reporting agent with read access to Salesforce, write to SharePoint, output to Slack
Agent skills: 'Add skill' in agent view; packages instructions, resources, and scripts

stats.fact: Workspace agents free research preview ends May 6, 2026; credit-based pricing begins on that date
60+ built-in enterprise connectors available for workspace agents (Slack, Google Drive, Salesforce, Notion, Atlassian Rovo, Microsoft apps, SharePoint, and more)
ChatGPT Business plan starts at $20 per user per month; Enterprise and Edu plans are variably priced
Workspace agents are off by default for Enterprise customers at launch and require admin activation
Workspace agents explicitly replace Custom GPTs, which were introduced in late 2023
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard now shared by OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google for tool calling
Workspace agents launched April 22, 2026 in research preview for Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans

angles: Custom GPTs are dead — workspace agents are the new standard for teams using ChatGPT
Free until May 6, 2026: everything you need to set up and test before pricing kicks in
The three-pillar advantage: background execution, org-wide sharing, and real SaaS actions vs old Custom GPTs
60+ integrations deep-dive: which connectors matter most for engineering, sales, and finance teams
Enterprise security first: how admins can govern, monitor, and kill-switch workspace agents
Building skills: how to create reusable agent 'programs' that any teammate can deploy
Slack-first teams: running ChatGPT workspace agents entirely inside your existing Slack channels
MCP custom connectors: extending workspace agents beyond the 60 built-in integrations
